prompt: A powerful editorial visual metaphor about global income inequality. A vast landscape where the ground is dramatically tilted like a steep slope. At the top of the slope stands a single luxurious golden mansion overflowing with wealth, gold coins, jewels, and luxury objects. Far below, thousands of small worn houses and crowded communities cluster together with almost nothing. Wealth objects visibly slide uphill toward the mansion, emphasizing extreme imbalance and systemic unfairness. Cinematic wide-angle perspective looking up the slope, dramatic lighting, strong contrast between rich golden light above and muted grey poverty tones below. Minimalist but emotionally powerful symbolism. Long-form magazine opener illustration. Clear visual storytelling without labels, charts, graphs, legends, numbers, or text. No bar charts, no pie charts, no line graphs, no infographic layout, no dashboards, no floating numbers.
dataset_name: World Inequality Database – Global Wealth Distribution
insight: The richest 1% hold more wealth than billions of people combined.
concept: Extreme wealth concentration in which resources accumulate toward a tiny elite while billions remain with very little.
metaphor: A tilted landscape where wealth slides uphill toward a single mansion, symbolizing how the economic system channels resources toward the richest few.
